item.title: Gilead's Long-Acting HIV Pill Trial
item.description:
• Gilead has ended a Phase 2/3 trial evaluating its long-acting oral HIV pill regimen. The discontinuation follows the FDA's decision to maintain a clinical hold on the study. The trial was assessing a combination of investigational agents, lenacapavir and islatravir, aimed at providing a once-weekly oral treatment option. The hold and subsequent trial termination highlight ongoing development challenges in the long-acting HIV therapeutic space.
  • Lenacapavir: Capsid inhibitor. Islatravir: Nucleoside reverse transcriptase translocation inhibitor (NRTTI).
  • Endpoints News, Fierce Biotech
